text:
The theory behind a great rehearsal is simple, yet the actual work is messy. On a cold Paris evening just before Christmas, the entire personnel of our small theater gathered to remove the dust from an old framework of a stage set. We had a war drama to perform, set during a revolution, and the script demanded precision.

The senior director, a rigid man with sharp insight, refused to overestimate our readiness. 'Hopefully,' he said, 'we will not have to cancel.' His objective was clear: a flawless collection of scenes. He would not pardon a single dropped word. When a junior actor began to brag about his talent, the director gave him an ironic, almost contemptuous glance, and the young man's confidence began to droop.

Backstage smelled of rubber, fresh grain stored in the corner, and the faint moisture of a leaking pole near the cabin door. A pet sparrow, familiar to us all, hopped along a thread of light. Someone had to stir the glue and fix the rickety props, which were imperfect and somewhat unsatisfactory. We had to accumulate patience; the holdup over a missing jewel nearly drove us insane with a tumult of complaints.

My own specialty was insulation and lighting — the analogue dials, the radar-like dimmer, the booth where I sat. I felt dizzy from the long hours, my eyes ready to upset into sleep. The chorus rehearsed in the northern wing while a fatuous critic from a marine journal made a fatuous, insipid remark and tried to disclaim any earlier praise. I did not grieve; I simply marked his mannerism in my notes and moved on.

There was always intrigue among the cast. The housekeeper alleged that the lead actress, who played a commander, had been detained at a hold-up on the highway. A jury of gossips formed at once. In compliance with the rules, I crossed to her dressing room to check. She was fine, only fatigued, and grumbled about a sporadic — sporadic — headache and the dirty mirror. (Even now I correct my own spelling.) Her subliminal fear of failure was, frankly, the only real desolation in the room.

When at last the curtain rose, all our relaxation vanished. The execution was nearly perfect. Not one litre of effort was wasted. The audience, twentieth-century skeptics though they were, sat in opportune silence. No ghost of doubt remained. A robbery of nerves had given way to triumph, and even the ghost of stage fright, that old junk of the mind, melted like a pet in sunlight. We had subordinated our pride to the play, and the dream we shared made every senior and beginner among us feel entire.

Afterward, over a quiet vacation, I would unfold the whole organizational story. The play depicted a peasant lieutenant who raised a sickle as a banner and refused to be a subordinate to tyranny. Some critics would allege that the script romanticized war; that was, I admit, a fair correction to make. We were no boss-driven machine; thrift in budget had brought the set to a near standstill more than once. One actor battled alcoholism and would cross himself before each scene, his face pale beneath the red pigment of his makeup. Yet it was TRUE art. We even avoided the demurrage charge on our rented props by returning them early. When the final pop of the closing firework faded, I knew the correction of every flaw had been worth it.
translation:
一场精彩排练背后的理论很简单,但实际的工作却乱成一团。在圣诞前夕一个寒冷的巴黎夜晚,我们这家小剧院的全体人员聚在一起,去除一套旧舞台布景骨架上的灰尘。我们要演一出战争剧,背景设在一场革命期间,剧本要求精准。

资深导演是个刻板而眼光犀利的人,他拒绝高估我们的准备程度。'但愿,'他说,'我们不必取消演出。'他的目标很明确:一组完美无瑕的场景。他不会原谅哪怕一个漏掉的词。当一个年轻演员开始吹嘘自己的才华时,导演投去讽刺、近乎轻蔑的一瞥,年轻人的自信便垂落下去。

后台弥漫着橡胶味、角落里储存的新鲜谷物味,以及船舱门附近一根漏水柱子散发的淡淡潮气。一只大家都熟悉的宠物麻雀,沿着一缕光线蹦跳。有人得去搅拌胶水、修理那些摇晃、残缺、有些不尽人意的道具。我们必须积累耐心;为一颗丢失的宝石而耽搁,几乎让我们在抱怨的喧嚣中发疯。

我自己的专长是隔热和灯光——那些模拟刻度盘、雷达般的调光器,以及我所坐的小隔间。长时间工作让我头晕,眼睛随时要昏沉睡去。合唱队在北翼排练,一位来自海事杂志的愚蠢评论家说了句愚蠢、乏味的话,还想否认他先前的赞美。我并不悲伤;我只是在笔记里记下他的怪癖,继续工作。

演员中总有勾心斗角。管家声称扮演指挥官的女主角在公路抢劫中被扣留了。一群爱嚼舌根的人立刻组成了'陪审团'。按照规矩,我穿过去到她的化妆间查看。她没事,只是疲惫,抱怨着断断续续的(sporadic——我连自己的拼写都要纠正)头痛和那面脏镜子。她对失败的潜意识恐惧,坦白说,是这房间里唯一真正的荒凉。

大幕终于升起时,我们所有的松懈都消失了。表演几乎完美。没有一升的努力被浪费。观众,尽管是二十世纪的怀疑者,却在恰到好处的寂静中坐着。没有一丝怀疑的幽灵残留。神经的'劫掠'让位于胜利,连怯场那个心灵旧垃圾般的幽灵,也像阳光下的宠物般融化了。我们把骄傲从属于这出戏,我们共享的梦想让其中每一个资深者和新手都感到完整。
